steps: The triangle inequality theorem requires that each pair of sides sums to more than the third side. With sides 6, 12, and \(x\), apply all three conditions.
Condition 3: \(12 + x > 6\) gives \(x > -6\), which is always true for a positive length. Combining: \(6 < x < 18\).
choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '\(x < 18\)': This gives only the upper bound but ignores the lower bound \(x > 6\). For example, \(x = 1\) satisfies \(x < 18\) but does not form a valid triangle because \(6 + 1 = 7 < 12\). ✗
  • Choice B — '\(x > 18\)': This is the opposite of the correct upper bound. A side greater than 18 would violate \(6 + 12 > x\). ✗
  • Choice C — '\(6 < x < 18\)': Captures both the lower bound (\(x > 6\) from \(6 + x > 12\)) and the upper bound (\(x < 18\) from \(6 + 12 > x\)). All three triangle inequality conditions are satisfied. ✓
  • Choice D — '\(x < 6\) or \(x > 18\)': These are exactly the values that do NOT form a valid triangle — they violate at least one inequality. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
